mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero (0.09% as of 2011-06), the near-total absence of a risk-free return alternative compresses equity discount rates and sustains a persistent bid for equities via portfolio rebalancing out of cash and short-duration assets, producing a positive drift in S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage_value=0.09 as of 2011-06-01, effectively zero lower bound
regime_hint: zero lower bound / quantitative easing era
failure_condition: If mean 20-day forward SPX return during ZLB months (FEDFUNDS<0.25%) is not statistically greater than the full-sample mean (t-test p>0.05) over any rolling 2-year window, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A discrete improvement in UNRATE (decline of at least 0.2 percentage points over two consecutive monthly readings) signals accelerating labor market healing that raises consensus earnings and consumption forecasts, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by >=0.2pp over 2 consecutive monthly readings
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE revised_value=9.1 down from vintage_value=9.2 as of 2011-06-01
failure_condition: If triggered instances show negative or flat median 20-day forward returns (<=0) in more than 40% of occurrences across the 2005-2024 sample, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive 3-month momentum in INDPRO and RSAFS indicates synchronized expansion of production and consumption, a joint confirmation of demand-supply alignment that is a stronger positive signal for forward equity returns than either series alone, because it rules out inventory-driven false positives.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change > 0 AND RSAFS 3-month change > 0 simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised_value=94.172 up from vintage_value=93.07 as of 2011-06-01
failure_condition: If the joint-trigger signal's 60-day forward return distribution is not significantly different from the single-series INDPRO-only signal (Kolmogorov-Smirnov p>0.10), reject the interaction claim.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) remaining depressed below 600 thousand units reflects continued household balance-sheet repair and weak residential investment, a leading indicator of subdued aggregate demand that suppresses forward equity returns during deleveraging recoveries, distinct from normal cyclical dips.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised_value=561.0 thousand units as of 2011-05-01, near post-crisis lows
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns during HOUST<600k regimes are positive in more than 55% of trading days, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 2% while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, realized real interest rates turn increasingly negative, incentivizing a reflationary rotation into equities as a store of value, producing positive forward returns that would not occur from either series in isolation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL revised_value=224.806 vs vintage_value=224.304 as of 2011-06-01 indicating rising price level
regime_hint: reflationary QE regime with negative real rates
failure_condition: If the joint negative-real-rate signal's mean 20-day return is not significantly higher than the FEDFUNDS-only ZLB signal (paired t-test p>0.05), reject the interaction hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in the 3-month average net change in PAYEMS below 100,000 jobs signals a stalling labor recovery that precedes downward revisions to growth and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average net change falls below 100,000 jobs
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS revised_value=131931.0 thousand vs vintage_value=131017.0 thousand as of 2011-06-01, reflecting modest job growth
failure_condition: If triggered instances of sub-100k payroll growth are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending, the largest component of GDP, and provides an always-on short-horizon signal of near-term corporate revenue strength that supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised_value=375587.0 million vs vintage_value=387789.0 million as of 2011-06-01
failure_condition: If the sign of month-over-month RSAFS growth shows a Rank IC below 0.03 with 5-day forward SPX returns over any rolling 2-year window, reject hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO's 6-month annualized growth rate below 2% signals a mid-cycle industrial growth scare, consistent with the 2011 soft-patch environment following supply disruptions, which raises recession-probability pricing and depresses S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 6-month annualized growth rate falls below 2%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised_value=94.172 as of 2011-06-01, indicating decelerating industrial momentum amid 2011 growth scare
regime_hint: mid-cycle growth scare / industrial soft patch
failure_condition: If SPX 60-day forward returns following sub-2% INDPRO growth triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, reject hypothesis.
